description: Persistent history of completed and past trips per rider and per driver, including tracking numbers, activity timelines, and position trails for audit and replay.

actors.name: Customer
actors.description: Views their past rides and trip summaries.

actors.name: Driver
actors.description: Views their completed trips and earnings history.

actors.name: Operator
actors.description: Audits trip history across all drivers and customers.

fields.name: order_id
fields.label: Public identifier of the completed or past order.

fields.name: tracking_number
fields.label: Alphanumeric tracking reference visible to customers.

fields.name: customer_id
fields.label: Reference to the customer who requested the trip.

fields.name: driver_id
fields.label: Reference to the driver who fulfilled the trip.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Final status of the trip (completed, canceled).

fields.name: started_at
fields.label: When the trip started (rider picked up).

fields.name: tracking_statuses
fields.label: Chronological list of activity status changes with timestamps and locations.

fields.name: position_trail
fields.label: Sequence of GPS positions recorded during the trip for route replay.

fields.name: limit
fields.label: Maximum number of results per page.

rules.rule_01: Trip history includes all orders associated with a given customer or driver, regardless of current status.
rules.rule_02: Queries can be filtered by status (completed, canceled), date range, customer ID, or driver ID.
rules.rule_03: Each order in history carries its tracking number, status, route summary, and timestamps.
rules.rule_04: Tracking statuses (activity log) are stored per order and ordered chronologically.
rules.rule_05: Position trail records are stored per order/driver and enable route replay with up to 50-meter resolution.
rules.rule_06: History is paginated; a limit and cursor/page parameter control the result window.
rules.rule_07: Operators can access all history within their organization; customers and drivers see only their own.

given: authenticated request to list orders filtered by driver_id

then.description: Paginated list of orders where driver is the assigned driver, with summary fields.

driver_history_retrieved.result: Driver sees their trip history with status, distance, duration, and timestamps.

given: authenticated request to list orders filtered by customer_id

then.description: Paginated list of orders linked to the customer.

customer_history_retrieved.result: Customer sees all their past rides with tracking numbers and final statuses.

given: request for tracking statuses for a specific order

then.description: Ordered list of activity events, each with code, status label, timestamp, and location.

tracking_timeline_retrieved.result: Full activity timeline for the trip is returned.

given: request for position history for a specific order and driver

then.description: Ordered GPS positions with heading, speed, and altitude recorded at 50-meter intervals.

position_trail_retrieved.result: Route of the trip can be replayed on a map.

given: requested order ID does not exist or belongs to a different organization

errors.message: The specified order could not be found.

related.reason: Every entry in trip history is a completed order from the lifecycle.
Position records that make up the position trail are written during location streaming.
Tracking statuses are written at each state machine transition.
Customer app exposes trip history to riders.
Driver app exposes trip history to drivers.

goals.description: Persistent history of completed and past trips per rider and per driver, including tracking numbers, activity timelines, and position trails for audit and replay.

constraints.description: Data consistency must be maintained across concurrent operations

tradeoffs.reason: data consistency must be maintained across all operations
